Fix: override sidebar-nav.html to use single-line nav_pages assignment

The remote theme's sidebar-nav.html used a multi-line Liquid assign
for nav_pages which caused Liquid 4.0.4 to assign nil, making
subsequent where_exp | sort chains fail with:
  'Cannot sort a null object. (ArgumentError)'

Override with a single-line assignment that is safe in all Liquid versions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tools): /workspace path alias → project workspace

Agents can now use /workspace/src/main.py instead of the internal
/app/data/workspaces/<project-id>/src/main.py path.

- Extract _resolve_file_path(path, project_path) as pure function
- /workspace  and /workspace/  →  project_path
- /workspace/<rest>  →  project_path/<rest>
- Other absolute paths (other projects, /tmp, /etc…) pass through unchanged
  (security enforcement stays in code_tools._is_path_allowed)
- 33 unit tests covering: good paths, other-project paths, system paths,
  path traversal attempt, edge cases (partial prefix, no project_path)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use hatchling build system for textual-autocomplete in flake.nix

textual-autocomplete uses hatchling as its build backend, not setuptools.
This caused 'BackendUnavailable: Cannot import hatchling.build' when
building the package via nix.

Also dynamically read version from pyproject.toml to avoid version drift.

Closes #31

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: enhance documentation and resolve audit findings

- Enhance internal/reporter/doc.go with comprehensive package documentation including detailed descriptions of all 5 output formats (Console, JSON, HTML, CSV, Markdown) with use cases, Reporter interface documentation, usage examples, and section filtering explanation. Coverage upgraded from 7 to 42 lines.

- Resolve 4 medium-priority audit findings in internal/reporter/AUDIT.md:
  - Package naming: Clarify that 'reporter' package name correctly matches directory (FALSE POSITIVE)
  - Documentation: Mark medium documentation issue as FIXED (enhanced doc.go)
  - Function placement: Explain that constructor functions follow Go idioms (WONTFIX)
  - API surface: Accept broad exports as designed for multi-format reporting library (ACCEPTED)

- Resolve 6 medium/low-priority audit findings in internal/storage/AUDIT.md:
  - Package naming: Clarify that 'storage' package name correctly matches directory (FALSE POSITIVE)
  - Code duplication: Refactor populateNullableFields/populateNullableStrings to use single generic helper, eliminating 6-line duplicate (FIXED)
  - Complexity: Mark matchesFilter refactoring as already resolved in earlier commit
  - API design: Accept 8-method MetricsStorage interface as naturally grouped (WONTFIX)
  - Package documentation: Mark as false positive (100% coverage confirmed)
  - Store/Retrieve complexity: Mark as already resolved (functions well under thresholds)
  - File organization: Accept current backend-type split as optimal (WONTFIX)

- Refactor internal/storage/sqlite.go:
  - Convert populateNullableStrings to generic helper accepting string pointers
  - Update populateNullableFields to use refactored helper
  - Update scanSnapshotInfo call signature for pointer arguments
  - Reduce code duplication and improve maintainability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): add description + triggers to all 120 skills (batch)

Stocktake revealed:
- 103/121 skills missing 'description' field
- 56/121 skills missing 'triggers' field

Batch fixes:
- description: derived from tagline+role, or first sentence of persona.description,
  or motivation. Max 120 chars. Role-specific wording preserved.
- triggers: 3 natural-language 'when X' entries per skill, matched to role family
  (dev/arch/security/data/product/qa/platform/rse/finops/incident/...).
  56 skills received triggers. Skills with existing triggers (65) untouched.

All 120 YAMLs parse cleanly. 0 errors.

WHY: description drives agent selection UI + stocktake verdicts.
     triggers enable auto-routing without manual agent assignment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: parseable ERR emission, named arg round-trip, \U escape support (#548)

- CalorEmitter: emit ERR "TODO: ..." instead of ERR{"TODO:..."} to prevent
  cascading Calor0100 parse errors (FallbackExpressionNode)
- CalorEmitter: emit named argument labels A[name] in Visit(CallStatementNode)
  and Visit(CallExpressionNode) when ArgumentNames are present
- Parser: add ParseArgument(out argumentName) overload that handles A[name]
  syntax for named arguments; collect names in both statement and expression
  call parsing
- Lexer: add \UXXXXXXXX (8-digit) Unicode escape support via
  AppendLongUnicodeEscape(), applied in both triple-quoted and regular strings
- Tests: 22 new tests in ConverterQualityTests.cs covering ERR parsing,
  Unicode escapes, named arg round-trip, and real-world patterns
- Update existing assertion in CSharpToCalorConversionTests for new ERR format

Addresses cascading parse failures reported in Newtonsoft.Json (269 failures)
and Humanizer (72 failures) conversion reports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: calor
repository.description: Calor - Coding Agent Language for Optimized Reasoning

commit.message:
fix: use force reset for submodule updates to avoid merge conflicts

These submodules are read-only upstream sources. Using --merge can cause
conflicts when upstream rewrites history or has divergent changes. Reset
each submodule to its remote HEAD before updating to avoid conflicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: keep action bar always mounted to prevent message width shrink on hover

The action toolbar child was being dynamically mounted/unmounted based on
hover state via UpdateActionChromeMount(). Since the toolbar sits in an
Auto-sized Grid column, removing its child caused the column to collapse
to 0 width, making the star-sized bubble column expand. On hover, the
child was re-added, restoring the Auto column width and shrinking the
bubble — causing a visible layout jump.

Fix: always keep the action bar child mounted. The XAML styles already
control visibility through Opacity and IsHitTestVisible on :pointerover,
:host-scrolling, and :editing pseudo-classes, so the mount/unmount
optimization is unnecessary and actively harmful to layout stability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(llm): return synthetic neutral response on Azure HTTP 400 content policy

When Azure OpenAI blocks a prompt with content management policy (HTTP
400), return a neutral '[CONTENT_POLICY_BLOCKED]' completion instead of
raising an exception that triggers circuit breaker + infinite retry loop.

Affects all three call paths: chat completions, Responses API, streaming.
Particularly relevant for the ac-adversarial agent whose adversarial
prompts sometimes trigger Azure content filters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove cache-first service worker causing stale pages

Root cause: sw.js used cache-first strategy — old HTML pages cached by SW
were served forever, ignoring new deployments. Users clearing browser cache
doesn't affect SW cache, so old landing page persisted across all deploys.

Fix:
- Replace sw.js with self-destructing version (clears all caches + unregisters)
- ServiceWorkerRegister now unregisters SW and clears caches on all visits
- Existing users' old SW will fetch new sw.js (skipWaiting) → auto-cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wire up homepage search to redirect to skills page

The homepage search bar had no event handler — typing and pressing Enter
did nothing. Now it redirects to /skills/?q=<query>. The skills page
also reads the q URL parameter to pre-fill and apply the filter on load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix pre-deploy blockers: packages, CORS, lifespan, task discovery

- requirements.txt: add fredapi==0.4.3, langgraph-checkpoint-postgres==2.0.8
- app/worker.py: import ingestion tasks at end of module (Celery auto-discovery)
- app/api/main.py: add lifespan handler (checkpointer.setup() on startup),
  CORSMiddleware (localhost:3000 + FRONTEND_URL), structlog JSON config for prod
- app/config.py: add FRONTEND_URL setting for production CORS
- .env.local.example: add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, FRONTEND_URL, model vars

All 168 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix custom field resolution for projects not in API member list

- extractProjectKeyFromJQL: add regex fallback to extract project key
  directly from JQL string (e.g. 'project = MYTUBE' or 'MYTUBE-123')
  when the project is not in the known project keys list (user not member)
- resolveFieldNamesForSearch: use first known project key as additional
  fallback when JQL pattern extraction also fails
- resolveFieldNamesForSearch: flatten comma-separated field values passed
  as a single CLI argument (e.g. 'key,summary,Diagrams' as one string)
- Update test to reflect improved regex-based extraction behavior

The root cause was that /project endpoint only returns projects where
the API user is a member. When querying projects like MYTUBE where the
user has browse-only access, extractProjectKeyFromJQL returned empty,
causing resolveFieldNamesForSearch to skip field resolution entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: Rewrite 06_add_people_from_wikipedia.py to eliminate LLM dependency

Reduces Mistral API calls from 100+ per run to at most 1 optional call.
Script now works entirely without Mistral using Wikipedia + spaCy NER.

Key changes:
- Wikipedia search + spaCy NER for entity discovery (was: multiple Mistral calls)
- Regex/heuristics for bio data extraction (was: LLM extraction)
- spaCy NER for relationship/institution extraction (was: LLM extraction)
- Simple heuristic validation: Wikipedia page exists = valid (was: LLM scoring)
- Reduced from 2619 to ~1854 lines

Preserved: PersonEntity/InstitutionEntity/RelationshipDetail classes,
file creation logic, Wikidata/HATVP enrichment, exploration report,
git commit, configurable limits, GITHUB_ACTIONS detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor+feat: evolve→YAML write, promote instincts, dedup _run, dead code

refactor(tools/dep_tools): remove duplicate async _run(), reuse _helpers.run_proc
  - dep_tools.py imported _helpers.run_proc as _run (identical implementation)
  - Removed local copy + unused asyncio import + TIMEOUT constant
  - Completes pattern: lint_tools + type_check_tools already used _run_base

feat(instinct): evolve writes YAML to disk + promote project→global
  - evolve_instincts(): now writes skill YAML to platform/skills/definitions/
    and returns yaml_path in response (circuit closed: observe→instinct→skill file)
  - promote_global_instincts(): new function — promotes project-scoped instincts
    to global scope when seen in 2+ projects with confidence ≥ 0.7
  - observe_session(): auto-fires promote_global_instincts after each session
    that generated new instincts (non-blocking, errors ignored)

feat(api/instincts): /evolve now works without agent_id (all agents)
  - POST /api/instincts/evolve with no agent_id → evolves all eligible agents
  - Returns {success, clusters: [{skill_id, yaml_path, ...}]}
  - New POST /api/instincts/promote → manual trigger for global promotion
  - instincts router now exposes 7 endpoints

SOURCE: ECC continuous-learning-v2 — closed the full learning loop:
  session observations → instincts → skill YAML files on disk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: JsonElement Object/Array should not convert to string in SpecReviewEvaluator

When parsing manualVerificationItems from condition metadata, TryConvertToString
was incorrectly converting JsonElement objects (array items) to their JSON string
representation via the default case. This caused ParseManualVerificationItem to
treat the whole JSON object as the label string instead of extracting label/reason
properties.

Fix: add explicit case for JsonValueKind.Object|Array to return false, allowing
the property extraction logic to run correctly.

Resolves F-080-C1~C6: spec repository config (.flow/config.json) implementation
is already present; this fixes the failing SpecReviewEvaluatorTests test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
